message.reasoning:
The user wants Base Runtime Instructions: staged reading strategy, reply JSON only.

We have task: Gather exact evidence needed before editing schema definitions so fixtures etc. are versioned. This is a prepare_to_edit command.

We have graph relations showing symbols in artifacts.rs. We need to find relevant schema version constants. Possibly they need to get exact definitions of constants like FIXTURE_MANIFEST_SCHEMA_VERSION, RUN_MANIFEST_SCHEMA_VERSION, etc. They may need to hydrate or expand file to see the values. The history shows they already performed some expansions/hydrations but still no evidence obtained? It seems they haven't yet retrieved the actual values. So we need to perform an action to get exact values.

We can use session.hydrate or session.expand_file to get more context. The recommended frontier action is expand_file [F1] priority=112 candidates=20 :: Expand file symbols for artifacts.rs. So we probably need to call session.expand_file with target selector referencing the file.

The selector likely is something like "file:crates/graphbench-core/src/artifacts.rs". In this system, short ids: F1 seems to be the file node? Actually earlier they list graph relations: [F1] crates/graphbench-core/src/artifacts.rs --exports--> [S1] ... So F1 is the file node. So to expand symbols, we use session.expand_file with target selector maybe "F1". The payload likely includes target selector and maybe depth etc. The tool name is session.expand_file@v1.

Thus we should make a tool_call of kind "tool_call" with tool_name "session.expand_file" and payload includes target selector maybe "F1". Also maybe set depth etc? Not required but can include minimal.

We also need to include acquired_fact_ids list; maybe empty initially. readiness_state likely "evidence_visible" after expansion? But we haven't acquired evidence yet; we just request expansion. The readiness might be "not_ready" until we get evidence. However the contract says readiness_state can be "not_ready|evidence_visible|evidence_acquired|ready_to_edit". Since we haven't yet retrieved evidence, maybe "not_ready". But we are about to call expand_file to get evidence. So readiness_state could be "not_ready". The assistant_message should be brief status.

Thus output JSON:

{
  "kind":"tool_call",
  "assistant_message":"Expanding symbols in artifacts.rs to locate schema version constants",
  "tool_call":{
    "tool_name":"session.expand_file@v1",
    "payload":{
      "target":"F1",
      "depth":2,
      "limit":20
    }
  },
  "acquired_fact_ids":[],
  "readiness_state":"not_ready"
}

But need to follow exact shape: tool_call.payload must be an object; we can include any fields recognized by tool. The description says session.expand_file payload includes target selector and optional depth, limit, relation filters, priority threshold, traversal budgets. So we can include "target":"F1", "depth":2, "limit":20.

Make sure no extra fields. Also ensure assistant_message is brief.

Thus final answer.
